question: [File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/token/Token.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Annotation: @Upsertable(history = true, skipPartialUpdate = true)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a network partition where `skipPartialUpdate = true` causes a partial token update (e.g., only `pauseStatus` changed) to be silently skipped on a reconnecting node because the upsert logic treats the incoming record as a partial update, causing the node to permanently miss the state change?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/token/Token.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Annotation: @Upsertable(history = true, skipPartialUpdate = true)] During a network partition, can an unprivileged user cause the `skipPartialUpdate` logic to incorrectly classify a full token update as a partial update on a reconnecting node (due to missing fields in the partition-delayed message), causing the update to be silently dropped and the mirror node to serve stale token state indefinitely?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/token/Token.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Annotation: @Upsertable(history = true, skipPartialUpdate = true)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a partition where the `history = true` flag causes a `TokenHistory` record to be written on one node but not another, causing the two nodes to have different history depths for the same token, breaking historical balance or state queries that depend on complete history?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/token/Token.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Field: totalSupply, Annotation: @UpsertColumn] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a partition where the coalesce expression `case when {0} >= 0 then {0} else e_{0} + coalesce({0}, {1}) end` is evaluated with a `NULL` `e_totalSupply` (because the token was created during the partition on one node), causing `coalesce({0}, {1})` to fall back to a default value and produce an incorrect `totalSupply` after healing?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/token/Token.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Field: totalSupply, Annotation: @UpsertColumn] During a network partition, can an unprivileged user cause the `@UpsertColumn` coalesce expression to be applied to a `totalSupply` value that was already updated by a previous upsert on the same node, effectively double-applying a delta and causing `totalSupply` to diverge from the consensus value by exactly the delta amount?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/token/Token.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Field: treasuryAccountId] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a network partition where a treasury account update is processed by one node but not another, causing the two nodes to disagree on which account holds the treasury role for a token, enabling an attacker to route queries to the node with the stale `treasuryAccountId` to misrepresent token ownership?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/token/Token.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Field: treasuryAccountId, Converter: EntityIdConverter] During a network partition, can an unprivileged user cause the `EntityIdConverter` to convert a `treasuryAccountId` differently on two nodes (e.g., due to shard/realm encoding differences in a partition-delayed message), causing the two nodes to store different `EntityId` values for the same treasury account, permanently corrupting treasury account references?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/token/Token.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Field: maxSupply] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a network partition where a `maxSupply` field (marked `@Column(updatable = false)`) is written with different values on two nodes during token creation (if creation messages are duplicated across partition segments), causing the two nodes to enforce different supply caps for the same token?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/token/Token.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Field: initialSupply] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a partition where the `initialSupply` field (marked `@Column(updatable = false)`) is written on one node but not another during token creation, causing one node to have a `NULL` `initialSupply` and another to have the correct value, breaking supply validation logic in downstream consumers?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/token/Token.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Field: decimals] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a network partition where the `decimals` field (marked `@Column(updatable = false)`) is written with different values on two nodes (due to duplicate creation messages with different content), causing the two nodes to apply different decimal precision to token amounts, enabling precision-based accounting attacks?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/token/Token.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Field: type] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a partition where the `type` field (stored as `@Enumerated(EnumType.STRING)` with `@JdbcTypeCode(SqlTypes.NAMED_ENUM)`) is written with a `FUNGIBLE_COMMON` value on one node and `NON_FUNGIBLE_UNIQUE` on another (due to message corruption during partition), causing the two nodes to apply completely different token semantics to the same `tokenId`?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/token/Token.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Field: supplyType] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a partition where the `supplyType` field (stored as `@Enumerated(EnumType.STRING)`) is written as `FINITE` on one node and `INFINITE` on another, causing one node to enforce a `maxSupply` cap while the other does not, enabling supply-cap bypass in downstream systems that rely on mirror node data?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/token/Token.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Field: freezeDefault] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a partition where the `freezeDefault` field (marked `@Column(updatable = false)`) is written as `true` on one node and `false` on another during token creation, causing new token associations to be treated as frozen on one node and unfrozen on another, breaking freeze enforcement for all new accounts associating with that token?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/token/Token.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Field: createdTimestamp] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a partition where the `createdTimestamp` field (marked `@Column(updatable = false)`) is written with different values on two nodes (due to clock skew between partitioned nodes), causing the two nodes to disagree on the token's creation time, breaking time-based queries and historical state reconstruction?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/token/Token.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Field: tokenId] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a network partition where the same `tokenId` is used for two different token creation events (one on each partition segment), causing the two nodes to store completely different token records under the same `@Id`, and upon healing, one record silently overwrites the other, destroying one token's state?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/token/Token.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Field: feeScheduleKey] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a partition where a fee schedule key update is processed by one node but not another, causing the two nodes to have different `feeScheduleKey` byte arrays, so that fee schedule validation produces different results on different nodes, enabling fee bypass on the stale node?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/token/Token.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Field: freezeKey] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a network partition where a freeze key rotation is processed by one node but not another, causing one node to validate freeze transactions against the old key and another against the new key, creating a window where freeze operations are accepted by one node and rejected by another?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/token/Token.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Field: kycKey] During a network partition, can an unprivileged user cause a KYC key rotation to be applied on one node but not another, so that KYC grant/revoke transactions are validated against different keys on different nodes, causing KYC status to diverge and allowing unauthorized KYC grants to be accepted by the stale-key node?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/token/Token.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Field: supplyKey] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a partition where a supply key rotation is processed by one node but not another, causing one node to accept mint/burn transactions signed with the old key and another to require the new key, enabling unauthorized minting on the stale-key node during the partition window?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/token/Token.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Field: wipeKey] During a network partition, can an unprivileged user cause a wipe key rotation to be applied on one node but not another, so that wipe transactions are validated against different keys on different nodes, enabling unauthorized token wipes to be accepted by the stale-key node?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/token/Token.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Field: pauseKey] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a partition where a pause key rotation is processed by one node but not another, causing one node to accept pause/resume transactions signed with the old key and another to require the new key, enabling unauthorized pause or resume operations on the stale-key node?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/token/Token.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Field: metadataKey] During a network partition, can an unprivileged user cause a metadata key rotation to be applied on one node but not another, causing the two nodes to validate metadata update transactions against different keys, enabling unauthorized metadata updates to be accepted by the stale-key node?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/token/Token.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Field: metadata] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a partition where a metadata update is processed by one node but not another, causing the two nodes to serve different `metadata` byte arrays for the same token, breaking NFT metadata integrity for consumers that query different mirror nodes?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/token/Token.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Field: totalSupply] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a partition where a token wipe (which decrements `totalSupply`) is processed by one node and a mint (which sets `totalSupply` to a new absolute value) is processed by another, so that upon healing the positive-branch assignment in `setTotalSupply()` overwrites the wipe delta, causing the mirror node to report an inflated supply?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/token/Token.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: setTotalSupply()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a partition where the `totalSupply` field is `null` on a reconnecting node (token was never fully initialized due to partition during creation), causing the negative-branch to set `totalSupply = newTotalSupply` (a negative value) rather than aggregating, permanently corrupting the supply baseline?
